hook: I spent $89 on a dorm chair. Worth it?

scenes.visual_prompt: Static wide shot of a bare cinderblock dorm room, twin XL mattress still in plastic wrap, 11 cardboard boxes stacked by the door
scenes.voiceover: This was my sister's dorm room last August.

scenes.visual_prompt: Hands unboxing a folding velvet desk chair and two rigid storage bins from one shipping box
scenes.voiceover: I found one set that's a velvet desk chair AND under-bed bins for $89 shipped.

scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of blush velvet fabric texture on the folding X-frame chair, then chair unfolding flat for transport
scenes.voiceover: It's real velvet, folds flat, and actually looks like furniture instead of a plastic desk chair.

scenes.visual_prompt: Sister sitting in the chair at the dorm desk, timer overlay showing 20 minutes
scenes.voiceover: Padding held up for setup logistics, but I wouldn't call it an ergonomic chair for six-hour study sessions.

scenes.visual_prompt: Overhead shot sliding two rigid bins under a twin XL bed frame, tape measure showing 2 inches of clearance
scenes.voiceover: Both bins slid right under the bed and swallowed winter clothes, extra bedding, and a box of shoes.

scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up on the bin zipper being pulled, slight fraying visible at the pull tab
scenes.voiceover: These are the weak point long-term — I'd expect 1 to 2 years of daily use, not five.

scenes.visual_prompt: Final wide shot of the finished desk corner with chair and clear floor
scenes.voiceover: For a chair and hidden storage in one box, that's a trade I'd make again.

caption: The $89 combo that did more work than everything else in the car. Full breakdown (plus the exact links) on the blog — I go through what I'd skip too.
